seoTitle: Carlotta vs Carnamah — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Carlotta and Carnamah. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Carlotta and Carnamah.
heading: Carlotta vs Carnamah — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Carlotta, Western Australia and Carnamah, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Carnamah has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Carlotta (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Carlotta or Carnamah safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Carlotta has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Carnamah scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Carlotta scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Carnamah scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Carlotta or Carnamah?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Carnamah has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
